data.title: Chicago Fed's Goolsbee cautions against front-loading rate cuts - report
data.description:
Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago President Austan Goolsbee warned that the U.S. central bank should not rush into multiple interest-rate cuts, citing lingering inflation concerns despite a cooling jobs market, according to a Wednesday media report.

Goolsbee supported the policy-setting Federal Open Market Committee's decision last week to cut rates for the first time this year, he said in an interview with the Financial Times. He backed the FOMC move due to signs that U.S. President Donald Trump's tariffs were not proving as inflationary as feared.

Going forward, though, “I’m uncomfortable with overly frontloading a lot of rate cuts on the presumption that [inflation] will probably just be transitory and go away,” Goolsbee said, pointing to concerns across Midwest firms that inflation still was not tamed.

The inflationary impact of tariffs would likely be short-lived, the Chicago Fed chief told the FT, though inflation has surpassed the Fed's 2% goal for 4.5 years and was "now heading the wrong way."

data.title: Nexstar holds 'productive' talks with Disney amid Kimmel-Kirk comments controversy
data.description:
Nexstar Media (NASDAQ:NXST), which has preempted Jimmy Kimmel's talk show on its ABC-affiliated local television stations, said it has held "productive" talks with Disney (NYSE:DIS) in the wake of the comedian's remarks on slain political activist Charlie Kirk.

"We are engaged in productive discussions with executives at The Walt Disney Company, with a focus on ensuring the program reflects and respects the diverse interests of the communities we serve," Nextsar said on Wednesday.

The company, which is the largest local television station owner in the United States, along with rival Sinclair (NASDAQ:SBGI), preempted the show even before ABC suspended Kimmel for his comments.

Kimmel's suspension lasted only for six days after advocates from both sides of the political aisle disagreed with the move. Disney also witnessed a wave of subscription cancellations by users who viewed the action against the comedian to be harsh and as an attack on free speech.

Nexstar has called Kimmel's comments "ill-timed and insensitive" but is continuing to evaluate the status of Jimmy Kimmel Live! on its stations while keeping it preempted.

data.title: Cintas raises 2026 revenue guidance to $11.18B as management signals continued margin expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Cintas Corporation (CTAS) Q1 2026

Management View

  • CEO Todd Schneider highlighted first quarter revenue growth of 8.7% to $2.72 billion and stated, "Our ongoing investments continue to help drive revenue growth and expand margins." Schneider emphasized the strength of Cintas' value proposition and announced an increase in fiscal 2026 financial guidance.
  • Schneider reported, "First quarter total revenue grew 8.7% to $2.72 billion. The organic growth rate... was 7.8%. This is right where we like to be. Each of our three route-based businesses had strong revenue growth in the quarter." He added, "Gross margin as a percent of revenue was 50.3%, a 20 basis point increase over the prior year. Operating income grew to $617.9 million, an increase of 10.1% over the prior year. Diluted EPS of $1.20 grew 9.1% over the prior year."
  • Schneider revealed, "Reflecting our strong first quarter performance, we are raising our fiscal 2026 financial guidance. We expect our revenue to be in the range of $11.06 billion to $11.18 billion, a total growth rate of 7% to 8.1%. We expect diluted EPS to be in the range of $4.74 to $4.86, a growth rate of 7.7% to 10.5%."
  • James Rozakis, Executive VP & COO, stated, "Organic growth by business was 7.3% for Uniform Rental and Facility Services, 14.1% for First Aid and Safety Services, 10.3% for Fire Protection Services and Uniform Direct Sale declined 9.2%." He added that gross margin improvements were driven by "strategic sourcing by the supply chain team and process improvement initiatives from our engineering and Black Belt teams."
  • CFO Scott Garula reported, "First quarter operating income was $617.9 million compared to $561 million last year. Operating income as a percentage of revenue was 22.7%... Cash flow provided from operating activities was $414.5 million." Garula noted, "We also returned capital to shareholders via our quarterly dividends and announced an increase of 15.4% in our quarterly cash dividend. This marks the 42nd consecutive year that we increased our dividend."

Outlook

  • Schneider provided updated guidance, stating, "We expect our revenue to be in the range of $11.06 billion to $11.18 billion, a total growth rate of 7% to 8.1%. We expect diluted EPS to be in the range of $4.74 to $4.86, a growth rate of 7.7% to 10.5%."
  • Garula indicated, "Our guidance does not assume any future acquisitions, our guidance assumes a constant foreign currency exchange rate, the fiscal 2026 net interest expense of approximately $97.0 million, a fiscal 2026 effective tax rate of 20.0%, which is the same compared to our fiscal 2025."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the revenue and EPS guidance were both raised, and Schneider emphasized, "The implied growth in Qs 2 through 4 is higher than the opening guide at all points within the range."

Financial Results

  • Total revenue for Q1 2026 was $2.72 billion, with organic growth at 7.8%.
  • Gross margin as a percent of revenue was 50.3%, up 20 basis points year-over-year.
  • Operating income reached $617.9 million, increasing 10.1% over the prior year. Net income for Q1 was $491.1 million.
  • Diluted EPS was $1.20, a 9.1% increase over the prior year.
  • Cash flow provided from operating activities was $414.5 million. Capital expenditures were $102.0 million, and $347.4 million of shares were repurchased in the quarter.
  • Segment breakdowns showed strong organic growth in First Aid and Safety Services (14.1%) and Fire Protection Services (10.3%), while Uniform Direct Sale declined 9.2%.

Q&A

  • Manav Patnaik, Barclays: Asked about converting "no-programmers" during potential macro weakness and the impact of SAP implementation on Fire business margins. Schneider responded, "We help customers... to free up cash flow... we're confident we're able to continue to convert no-programmers..." On SAP, he said, "There are additional costs that come along with that. But we're quite bullish on that business, and we're investing for the future."
  • Keen Fai Tong, Goldman Sachs: Inquired about the selling environment and drivers for the guidance raise. Schneider replied, "There's nothing specific to call out. I wouldn't say there's any changes to sales cycles... Retention rates are still at very attractive levels. The customer base... was steady. If anything, I would say, improved slightly during the quarter." He added, "Our 3 route-based businesses are all performing very well, and we like the momentum."
  • Joshua Chan, UBS: Asked about vertical-specific customer behavior and EPS guidance range. Schneider reported, "We're not seeing really any change in behavior in each of the verticals. They are all accretive to our growth..." On guidance, "We've increased the guide at all points in the EPS within the range."
  • Andrew Wittmann, RW Baird: Sought clarity on First Aid segment margins and investments. Garula said, "We continue to invest in all of our route-based businesses..." Rozakis added, "Our gross margin for First Aid and Safety is actually flat sequentially... more of a timing issue."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised pointed questions about margin trends, macro risks, and competitive dynamics, reflecting a neutral to slightly positive sentiment as they acknowledged strong results but pressed for clarity on sustainability and segment-specific challenges.
  • Management maintained a confident and constructive tone, frequently stating, "We like the momentum we have in the business," and emphasizing ongoing investments. Confidence was also evident: "We are quite bullish on that business, and we're investing for the future."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone remained consistently confident, while analysts maintained a focus on growth sustainability and costs, mirroring prior calls.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue and EPS guidance were both raised this quarter, in contrast to last quarter's initial outlook.
  • Organic growth rates in Uniform Rental and Facility Services and First Aid and Safety moderated slightly compared to Q4 2025, but remained strong.
  • Management's confidence and emphasis on margin expansion and M&A pipeline were consistent, though the focus on sustained investment in technology and workforce capacity increased.
  • Analysts continued to focus on macro risks, segment profitability, and competitive positioning, maintaining a similar tone as the previous quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing investments in SAP for the Fire business as a source of additional costs impacting margins.
  • Tariff-driven expense growth remains a concern, but management pointed to supply chain diversification and efficiency initiatives as mitigation.
  • Analysts questioned the sustainability of margin improvements and the timing of returns on investments in technology and capacity.

Final Takeaway

Cintas management communicated confidence in the company's ability to deliver sustained organic growth, margin expansion, and increased shareholder returns in fiscal 2026. The raised guidance reflects strong demand across route-based businesses, targeted investments in technology and capacity, and an active M&A strategy. Management reiterated their focus on leveraging operational strengths and a broad customer base while acknowledging ongoing macroeconomic uncertainty and cost pressures, particularly in the Fire segment and from tariffs. The company remains positioned to pursue growth opportunities and maintain its competitive edge through disciplined investment and capital allocation.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Worthington Enterprises outlines margin expansion plans with targets above 30% as facility modernization advances
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Worthington Enterprises (WOR) Q1 2026

Management View

  • Joseph Hayek, President, CEO & Director, opened by noting a "very solid start" to the fiscal year, emphasizing "strong year-over-year growth in sales, adjusted EBITDA and earnings per share." He reported that sales in Q1 were up 18% over last year, with gross margin at 27.1%. Hayek highlighted, "Despite numerous headwinds, including cautious consumers and a hot summer that impacted outdoor activities and tariff costs and high interest rates...we grew our year-over-year adjusted EBITDA by 34%." He credited the company's results to its strategy and execution, emphasizing innovation, transformation, acquisitions, and the Worthington Business System.

  • New product launches such as Balloon Time Mini, A2L refrigerant cylinders, and Halo Griddles were cited as drivers of market share gains and growth in new markets. Hayek stated, "We've generated tremendous momentum with new product launches...These new products are enabling us to take market share, grow new markets and win new customers."

  • Hayek also discussed the successful integration of Elgen, acquired in June, and the ongoing positive impact of the 80/20 initiative in the water business, with plans to expand this initiative to other areas.

  • Colin Souza, VP & CFO, reported, "On a GAAP basis, we reported earnings of $0.70 per share compared to $0.48 per share in the prior year quarter." Adjusted earnings were $0.74 per share, up from $0.50, and consolidated sales for the quarter were $304 million. Souza highlighted the "$2.2 million purchase accounting charge related to inventory acquired from Elgen" as a factor impacting the quarter. He noted, "On a trailing 12-month basis, adjusted EBITDA now stands at $280 million with a TTM adjusted EBITDA margin of 23.3%." He also commented on strong cash flow and disciplined capital allocation, including $13 million in capital expenditures, $9 million in dividends, and repurchasing 100,000 shares.

Outlook

  • Management reiterated its commitment to margin expansion, with Hayek referencing a goal "to get gross margin over 30% and to have our SG&A as a percentage of sales at 20 or less." Souza added, "We feel like we're still on track with that, and our initiatives are driving some momentum and want that trend to continue."

  • Hayek noted that while many end markets continue to face headwinds, "we're performing very well and believe our best days are ahead of us."

Financial Results

  • Q1 consolidated sales reached $304 million, with an 18% increase compared to the prior year quarter. Gross profit rose to $82 million, and gross margin expanded to 27.1%. Adjusted EBITDA for the quarter was $65 million, with a 21.4% margin.

  • In Consumer Products, Q1 sales were $119 million, up 1% year-over-year. Adjusted EBITDA was $16 million, with a 13.6% margin. The segment faced lower gross margin due to tariff charges and lower volumes.

  • Building Products saw Q1 sales of $185 million, up 32% year-over-year, with Elgen contributing $21 million. Adjusted EBITDA for the segment was $58 million, with a margin of 31.3%. WAVE contributed $32 million in equity earnings, while ClarkDietrich provided $6 million.

  • Cash flow from operations was $41 million, free cash flow $28 million, and long-term funded debt closed at $306 million with $167 million in cash.

Q&A

  • Kathryn Thompson, Thompson Research Group, LLC, asked about margin drivers in Building Products. Hayek responded that "it's really a story of really nice execution in markets that are normalizing and normalized," with strong growth in heating, cooking, cooling, and construction businesses. He highlighted that the Paducah, Kentucky facility produced 900,000 A2L refrigerant cylinders last year, nearly triple the prior year.

  • Thompson inquired about WAVE's performance and sustainability. Souza commented, "WAVE continues to perform very well, up year-over-year in terms of equity contribution...their end markets, in particular, when they serve areas like education, health care, transportation, data centers, those are still very healthy, very strong."

  • Dan Moore, CJS Securities, Inc., asked about organic growth and product expansion. Hayek explained that new products like Balloon Time Mini and Halo Griddles are expanding distribution, with recent wins at Walgreens and Walmart.

  • Brian McNamara, Canaccord Genuity Corp., queried about price versus volume growth. Hayek stated, "Volumes up in Building Products, price pretty stable. In Consumer, volumes were down, but mix shifted more heavily towards our celebrations business."

  • Susan Maklari, Goldman Sachs Group, Inc., asked about operational efficiencies and Elgen integration. Souza noted, "We piloted [80/20] in the water business about 7 months ago. It's going very well...we're starting to evaluate where could this apply next across our portfolio." On Elgen, Souza stated, "Integration, we believe we're 90 days in. It's going very, very well."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were generally positive, focusing on margin expansion, successful product launches, and the integration of Elgen. Questions reflected optimism about execution and market opportunities but acknowledged ongoing macro and consumer headwinds.

  • Management maintained a confident but measured tone, emphasizing execution, operational discipline, and the pursuit of margin goals. Hayek consistently highlighted strategy and innovation as differentiators: "We will continue to leverage...innovation, transformation and acquisitions to maximize both our near- and long-term success."

  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone this quarter was more focused on execution, cash conversion, and successful integration of recent acquisitions, with slightly increased emphasis on operational discipline amid persistent macro challenges.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Management reiterated their gross margin target above 30% and SG&A as a percentage of sales at 20% or less—consistent with language from the prior quarter but with greater detail this quarter regarding the timeline and initiatives.

  • Margin expansion in Building Products was again a highlight, but this quarter saw more discussion of specific new products and their impact on growth and market share.

  • Analysts' questions this quarter were more focused on segment-specific drivers, the impact of tariffs, and M&A pipeline, compared to broader macro and guidance questions last quarter.

  • Management's confidence in their strategies was maintained, with increased detail on operational initiatives such as the 80/20 process and facility modernization.

Risks and Concerns

  • Hayek identified "cautious consumers and a hot summer that impacted outdoor activities and tariff costs and high interest rates that are impacting residential and commercial repair, remodeling and construction activity" as ongoing challenges.

  • Management is mitigating risks with cost control, operational efficiency (80/20 initiative), and strategic product launches.

  • Analysts raised concerns about headwinds in consumer demand, tariff impacts, and seasonality in gross margins.

Final Takeaway

Worthington Enterprises began fiscal 2026 with strong growth and execution, highlighted by margin expansion and successful integration of recent acquisitions. Management remains focused on margin targets above 30%, operational discipline, and leveraging innovation and transformation to drive future growth. Strategic product launches and disciplined capital allocation position the company to capitalize on market opportunities while navigating ongoing macroeconomic and consumer headwinds.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Quanta Services raised to Buy at Jefferies as continued TAM expansion underpins growth
data.description:
Quanta Services (NYSE:PWR) +3.7% in Wednesday's trading as Jefferies upgraded the power infrastructure company to Buy from Hold with a $469 price target, raised from $398, citing the company's ability to capture a broader total addressable market primarily through expanding into data center opportunities.

Jefferies analyst Julian Dumoulin-Smith sees Quanta (NYSE:PWR) as "uniquely positioned to capture
solution-based data center partnerships, incremental renewable tailwinds, long-cycle 765kV transmission projects, and large-diameter pipeline recovery, supporting a high-teens EPS CAGR into the next decade," adding that the market is "underestimating both consistency and torque" after shares pulled back from summer highs.

Compared with specialty E&C peers, Quanta (PWR) stands out for its combination of consistent high-teens earnings per share growth, double-digit margins, and longer-term visibility, and relative to broader industrial/E&C names, it offers greater torque by riding multiple fast-growing secular trends across data centers, transmission, renewables, pipeline, and potential generation exposure, the analyst wrote.

data.title: Zscaler, Varonis, SailPoint among top cybersecurity stocks to own, JPMorgan says
data.description:
Zscaler (NASDAQ:ZS), Varonis (NASDAQ:VRNS) and SailPoint (NASDAQ:SAIL) are among the top cybersecurity stocks to own into the end of the year, investment firm J.P. Morgan said. 

“Within Network Security, demand remains elevated for SASE and Next Gen SIEM,” J.P. Morgan analyst Brian Essex wrote in a note to clients. “Meanwhile, Identity and Data Security continue to move up the priority stack as enterprises anticipate the impact GenAI and agentic adoption will have on attack surfaces and data estates. As we exit earnings season with this backdrop, we highlight VRNS, ZS, SAIL, and CLBT as our top picks supported by strong execution, healthy expectation management, improving fundamentals and reasonable valuation levels.”

For Varonis, Essex said the company is “well positioned” to accelerated into 2026, as it has the right technology at the right time to benefit from rising demand for data security. “Accordingly, we see potential for growth to accelerate to over 20% [year-over-year] next year, while consensus estimates reflect 18% y/y growth,” Essex explained.

Regarding Zscaler, Essex said it has improving fundamentals and is benefiting from secular tailwinds. “We view Zscaler as one of the best positioned vendors to benefit from acceleration of ZTNA and SASE demand in response to cloud migration/adoption and architectural shifts we are seeing across the Security Software landscape. The company is executing well as it laps sales organization disruption it encountered last year, with sales productivity improving into CY26. 

Lastly, SailPoint is seen as having strong execution, while identity demand remains high.

“We cited tailwinds driving elevated demand for Identity in our 2025 Outlook and Identity continues to move up the priority stack, while the investible universe of quality pure-play Identity stocks has recently been reduced to two,” Essex explained, adding that there are “multiple levers for upside ahead,” including its federal business, new/emerging product traction (improving attach rates), large deal traction, and easier comparisons. 

The firm also added Varonis to its Analyst Focus List, reiterated Cellebrite (CLBT) as a Focus List pick, and raised its price targets for Varonis, Cellebrite, JFrog (FROG) and Check Point Software (CHKP).

data.title: Sen. Warren urges Fed to scrap plan revamping grading system for big banks
data.description:
Sen. Elizabeth Warren urged the Federal Reserve to scrap its proposal to revamp its grading system for big banks, warning it threatens financial stability.

The Fed said in July it was considering revising its supervisory rating framework for grading large banks as "well managed."

"The proposal would undermine supervisory ratings andease the Fed’s enforcement posture towards big banks with supervisory deficiencies," the Massachusetts Democrat wrote in a letter addressed to Michelle Bowman, the Fed's vice chair for supervision.

Some 23 of 36 big banks were not rated as "well managed" in July, Warren noted, subjecting them to restrictions on some activities.

"But instead of forcefully addressing managerial problems through corrective enforcement actions, the Fed isproposing to artificially inflate the ratings," she said, in a move that would be "yet another "deregulatory action from President Trump’s financial regulators that puts our country’s financial and economic stability at risk."

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Bloom Energy sinks as Jefferies cuts, with valuation reflecting high expectations amid uncertainty
data.description:
Bloom Energy (NYSE:BE) -11.7% in Wednesday's trading after Jefferies downgraded shares to Underperform from Hold with a $31 price target, citing limited visibility into post-2026 growth
and "some early signs of over-exuberance."

Bloom Energy (BE) is indeed a leader in a promising niche, with many bullish assumptions facing practical constraints, Jefferies analysts led by Dushyant Ailani said, but risks to the downside
outweigh further upside at the stock's current valuation  at ~31x estimated FY 2027 EBITDA, pricing in aggressive growth.

"It is tough to justify a 30x multiple with no visibility on what 2027 and beyond looks like," the analysts wrote, and applying a more reasonable multiple implies volume levels that "seem quite
difficult to achieve," with the biggest debate involving how quickly the company can sign deals - "as management has noted previously, it takes time to ink bigger deals. While we don't underestimate BE's ability to get to 1 GW-plus eventually, we don't see that playing out near term."

Besides a press release with substantive data points on deals, Jefferies sees the next catalyst for the stock coming in February 26 when Bloom Energy (NYSE:BE) discloses its backlog, but "until then, we question the support level of the stock."

data.title: U.S. stocks falls as tech sector struggles, rate-cut uncertainties rattle markets
data.description:
Wall Street's major indexes were lower on Wednesday, as U.S. technology stocks weighed on the markets and uncertainty persisted among investors.

The S&P 500 (SP500) was last -0.4% in afternoon trade, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was also -0.5%, and the Dow (DJI) was -0.3%.

“Investors trimmed their exposure following a comment from Fed Chair Jerome Powell [saying] ‘equity prices are fairly highly valued.’ He went on to say that the current situation wasn’t a time ‘… of elevated financial stability risks,’ but investors decided that discretion is the better part of valour and took the opportunity to lighten up a touch,” said David Morrison, senior market analyst at Trade Nation.

On the tech front, OpenAI is considering renting AI chips from Nvidia (NVDA) -1% to reduce costs associated with the new $100B data center partnership between the two companies.

“This investment was welcomed in many quarters as evidence of the faith that the most valuable company in history has in the future of generative AI. But others have questioned the circular nature of the investment, particularly as the journey to monetizing the ongoing funding in AI is far from clear,” said Morrison.

“On top of this, the assumption is that Nvidia (NVDA), and other leading chip companies, have an unbridgeable moat around them. But as was demonstrated with DeepSeek at the beginning of this year, it’s quite possible that Chinese companies come up with cheaper alternatives to Nvidia’s products, particularly since U.S. bans on selling specific chips to China are driving Chinese innovation in this area,” he added.

Micron Technology (MU) edged lower -3.2%, even after its outlook eclipsed estimates on AI data center demand. Adobe (ADBE) -3% and Oracle (ORCL) -3% are also pushing the S&P 500 (SP500) and the Nasdaq (COMP:IND) lower.

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 4 basis points to 4.15%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 2 basis points to 3.61%. A $70B, 5-Year Note Auction slightly tailed.

On the economy, new home sales surged 20.5% MoM in August to 800k. Consensus was for 649k.

Business uncertainty about revenue growth rose to 3.83% in September, up from 3.77% in August, according to an Atlanta Fed survey. Also, CFOs recorded greater optimism in Q3, the Richmond Fed survey showed.

U.S. stocks ended in the red on Tuesday, breaking from their three-day all-time high closes, after Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell largely reiterated his message from last week that policymakers will have a difficult path ahead on interest rates.

data.title: Keurig Dr Pepper's plan to split business presents execution complexities -- Barclays
data.description:
Keurig Dr Pepper’s (NASDAQ:KDP) decision to split its beverage and coffee business will prove to be the right move, Barclays says, but the complex mechanics of the separation presents “elevated noise and uncertainty” over the next 12 months, leading analyst Lauren Lieberman to downgrade the stock to Equal Weight from Underweight and lower her price target by 33% to $26 to represent the sum-of-the-parts valuation.

Keurig Dr Pepper (NASDAQ:KDP) said it plans to separate its coffee and beverage units into two entities once its acquisition of JDE Peet's is complete. The coffee business will contain brands like Keurig and Green Mountain, and the newly acquired Peet’s Coffee, with ~$16B in annual net sales.

The beverage business will house brands including Dr Pepper, Canada Dry, and 7UP with over ~$11B in annual net sales.

“There has been minimal pushback from investors on the notion of this proposed division of assets making more sense than the current setup – the problem is really how we get there,” Lieberman says in her note to clients.

While there are lingering questions regarding the separation, Barclays’ Equal Weight rating reflects the balanced momentum in KDP’s beverage and coffee businesses with the uncertainty to come from the transaction.

“KDP’s fundamental setup no longer offers the compelling relative clarity that we had liked in the name previously,” Lieberman says, and that there will be some form of structural change to the beverage business post-separation given the different route-to-market and manufacturing models under one roof.

As for the coffee business, Lieberman likes the greater scale and diversification, but the combined business “still has its work cut out for it,” especially considering JDE Peet’s “checkered performance” since its 2020 IPO.

Lieberman’s revised price target (from $39) reflects 2% downside from KDP’s current share price, as “we see the uncertainty and complexity tied to the announced transactions as largely baked into the stock at this point,” she adds.

Since announcing the JDE Peet’s acquisition at the end of August, Keurig Dr Pepper (NASDAQ:KDP) shares have lost as much as 17% in value and continue to circle the drain at the 5 ½ year low.
